summary: P0 HOTFIX for authenticated PDF printing VERIFIED WORKING. The frontend openAuthenticatedPdf() helper is correctly wired to the OS 'Imprimir OS' button (data-testid='os-print-btn'). Click triggers axios GET /api/ordens-servico/{id}/pdf with Authorization: Bearer <token> header (proof: backend received the request with valid auth; response was NOT 401). No unauthenticated window.open occurred. However, a DIFFERENT backend bug was discovered: the PDF generation endpoint crashes with FPDFUnicodeEncodingException on OS records that have attachments, because line 3311 of server.py uses the em-dash character ("—") which isn't supported by FPDF's default helvetica/latin-1 font.

critical.issue: FPDFUnicodeEncodingException: Character '—' (em-dash) at index 62 not supported by helvetica (latin-1) font. Crashes whenever the OS has at least one attachment (attachments section renders the offending line). File: /app/backend/server.py:3311 -> pdf.cell(186, 5, f"  {fname} ({cat}) — {size_kb}KB"). Fix: replace '—' with '-' (ASCII hyphen) or switch FPDF to a Unicode TTF font. OS tested: 8d2b3a6c-ec46-4916-be1d-93ca7e09affa (has attachment 66b6dc10.jpeg).

action_items: Fix backend PDF em-dash unicode bug at /app/backend/server.py:3311 (replace '—' with '-' or add DejaVuSans TTF via pdf.add_font). This is preventing users from actually seeing the PDF even though the auth hotfix works.
Optional: after fixing the em-dash bug, re-run FLOW 1 to confirm blob: URL actually opens in new tab with rendered PDF content.
critical_code_review_comments: openAuthenticatedPdf() correctly maps 401/403/404/other to user-friendly Portuguese toasts and revokes the object URL after 120s + on beforeunload. Implementation is solid.
The URL.revokeObjectURL on 'beforeunload' of the new window is best-effort — some browsers may not fire beforeunload for blob: URLs; the setTimeout fallback (120s) is the safety net, which is fine.

Backend server.py line 3311 hard-codes an em-dash character which is incompatible with FPDF's default core fonts. Best practice: either (a) use ASCII-safe glyphs everywhere in FPDF strings when relying on core fonts, or (b) register a Unicode TTF (e.g. DejaVuSans) once at PDF creation and use unicode=True.
context_for_next_testing_agent: The authenticated-PDF hotfix (openAuthenticatedPdf helper) is verified working: axios sends Bearer token, no 401 occurs. Only remaining blocker is a backend FPDF unicode bug at server.py:3311 preventing the PDF blob from ever being returned successfully. After that server-side one-character fix, verify with FLOW 1 that a new tab opens with URL starting with 'blob:' and displays the PDF. Also verify FLOW for InspecoesPages.js line 631 (Inspeção print button) once the backend PDF works — I did not exercise that path due to economy mode.

reproduction: 3. Open any OS that has at least one attachment (e.g. #2026-00030).
5. Observe: axios GET /api/ordens-servico/{id}/pdf returns 500. Error toast appears; NO new tab opens; NO 401 (confirming hotfix works).
rca of the issue.root_cause: server.py line 3311 renders attachment metadata line using em-dash character '—' (U+2014). FPDF default helvetica font uses latin-1 which cannot encode U+2014, so PDF generation raises FPDFUnicodeEncodingException. This is a separate pre-existing backend defect unrelated to the auth hotfix; it just happens to also block the end-to-end verification of a rendered PDF.
rca of the issue.mitigation: Frontend hotfix already delivered and confirmed. Backend: change `f"  {fname} ({cat}) — {size_kb}KB"` to `f"  {fname} ({cat}) - {size_kb}KB"` (ASCII hyphen). Alternatively register a Unicode TTF font once with pdf.add_font('DejaVu','', '/usr/share/fonts/.../DejaVuSans.ttf', uni=True) and pdf.set_font('DejaVu','', 10).
hotfix_verification_evidence: Console: only 1 error, and it is 'Failed to load resource: 500' — NO 'Not authenticated' error.
Network: request URL is /api/ordens-servico/{id}/pdf with status 500 and response content-type application/json — proving axios was used (with Auth header) rather than window.open.
Backend log confirms user is authenticated (AUTH_OK test.admin@maintrix.com role=admin) and traces the 500 to a FPDF unicode error, not to auth middleware.
New tabs opened: 0 — because axios threw before URL.createObjectURL was reached, and the error-toast branch executed correctly.
